architecture.summary: SCPRM is a process reward model for knowledge graph question answering that decomposes reward estimation into cumulative past rewards and schema-aware future rewards. The cumulative past reward aggregates step-wise safety probabilities (via a risk head on LLM hidden states) using logarithmic summation to capture risk propagation without vanishing gradients. The future reward is computed by a schema head that measures the alignment gap between extracted query schema and induced reasoning schema, using a frozen embedding model as reference. Both components are trained via pairwise ranking losses on positive (correct, safe) and negative (incorrect or risky) reasoning paths. At inference, SCPRM serves as the value function in Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to guide multi-hop path exploration on knowledge graphs.

biblio.title: SCPRM: A Schema-aware Cumulative Process Reward Model for Knowledge Graph Question Answering

claims.statement: SCPRM addresses the risk compensation effect in process reward models by using multiplicative penalty accumulation via logarithmic summation of step-wise safety probabilities, ensuring risky steps impose persistent penalties rather than being offset by later correct steps

SCPRM overcomes length bias in conditional reward models by incorporating schema-aware future rewards that measure alignment between query schema and reasoning schema, rather than relying on multiplicative probability aggregation
On pairwise ranking accuracy with Llama3.1-8B base model, SCPRM achieves 95.34% on Medical KGQA compared to PRM baseline at 91.83%
On pairwise ranking accuracy with Llama3.1-8B base model, SCPRM achieves 88.11% on Legal KGQA compared to PRM baseline at 86.70%
SCPRM-MCTS with Llama3.1-8B achieves Hits@3 of 85.02% on Medical KGQA, outperforming ToG with GPT-4o-mini at 83.06% and ReKG-MCTS with GPT-4o-mini at 82.47%
SCPRM-MCTS with Llama3.1-8B achieves Hits@3 of 82.32% on Legal KGQA, outperforming ToG with GPT-4o-mini at 80.69% and ReKG-MCTS with GPT-4o-mini at 81.26%

Ablation study shows removing cumulative reward (w/o CR) causes pairwise ranking accuracy to drop by 9.85 to 11.82 percentage points across datasets, and Hits@k to drop by 9.46 to 12.15 percentage points

SCPRM exhibits graceful degradation under schema noise, maintaining robust performance even at 0.5 noise ratio without catastrophic failure, with larger models (Llama3.1-8B) showing enhanced robustness compared to smaller counterparts
